questions.Question_Text: Staff are aware of local mental health support services that they can direct participants to if required.
Awareness of the current needs and challenges facing each individual.
The organisation has strong partnerships with local schools and/or other public or social service providers.
Coaches have a clear protocol for who they can speak to within the organisation if they need advice or support for themselves or for a participant.
Coaches have received specific training relevant to working with the vulnerable groups in our community
Coaches have regular facilitated opportunities to debrief or exchange with other coaches or staff about their work.
We are experienced in running activities within the local community spaces of our participants.
We have digital communication channels in place for communicating with and motivating existing participants.
The organisation has strong partnerships with local community groups and organisations.
We have a diversity of income streams, and are not dependent on just one or two sources
We can support people who are financially disadvantaged to participate, e.g. waiving or subsidisng membership fees, providing sports kit/equipment and covering travel costs.
The organisation has strong partnerships with funders and/or sponsors.
The leadership team is representative of the diversity of the community it serves and decisions are made democratically.
We have processes for capturing the ideas and opinions of participants with regards to our sport club and these influence our decisions
The organisation has strong partnerships with, or is a member of, governance and advisory bodies both inside and outside the sport sector who they are accountable to.
We are experienced in running our activities outside of our main location and without our regular facilities.
We have digital tools and methods for running activities remotely for our participants.
The organisation has strong partnerships with other sport clubs and facility providers.
The organisation has strong partnerships with local schools and/or other public or social service providers.
Coaches have regular facilitated opportunities to debrief or exchange with other coaches or staff about their work.
The organisation has strong partnerships with local community groups and organisations.
The organisation has strong partnerships with funders and/or sponsors.
The organisation has strong partnerships with, or is a member of, governance and advisory bodies both inside and outside the sport sector who they are accountable to.
The organisation has strong partnerships with other sport clubs and facility providers.
